mechanism_narrative: Sustained employment growth, as indicated by increasing non-farm payrolls, signals a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion. This strength translates into higher consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with PAYEMS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals an accelerating economic contraction. This leads to reduced consumer confidence, lower discretionary spending, and declining corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales combined with stable or declining consumer price inflation indicates healthy consumer demand without significant cost pressures. This environment boosts corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < 2.5%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently high federal funds rate within a monetary tightening cycle increases borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and reducing the present value of future equity cash flows. This leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where FEDFUNDS is consistently above 4.5% for 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and accelerating retail sales reflect robust consumer confidence and spending, directly translating into higher corporate revenues and earnings. This positive momentum drives short-term investor optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of instances where RSAFS shows month-over-month growth exceeding 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signifies expanding manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating overall economic health and productive capacity. This positive supply-side momentum supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between INDPRO month-over-month growth and SPX 20-day returns is consistently negative or zero over any 4-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained recovery in housing starts indicates improving consumer and business confidence, increased construction activity, and broader economic recovery. This positive trend in a key cyclical sector drives investor optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month moving average exceeds 1500 thousand units
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where HOUST 3-month average is above 1500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power, increases input costs for businesses, and typically prompts tighter monetary policy from the central bank. This combination leads to reduced corporate profitability and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL month-over-month annualized change exceeds 4%.
